# elasticstack_fleet_cloud_connectors (Data Source)

Returns Fleet cloud connectors visible in a Kibana space. Cloud connectors are a preview feature in Kibana; this data source is experimental and may change in future provider releases. See the [Fleet Cloud Connectors API documentation](https://www.elastic.co/docs/api/doc/kibana/group/endpoint-fleet-cloud-connectors) for more information.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `kibana_connection` (Block List) Kibana connection configuration block. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--kibana_connection))
- `kuery` (String) Optional KQL filter passed to the Fleet API as the `kuery` query parameter.
- `page` (Number) Optional page number for API pagination, passed as the `page` query parameter.
- `per_page` (Number) Optional page size for API pagination, passed as the `perPage` query parameter.
- `space_id` (String) The Kibana space ID to scope the request to. When not specified, the default space is used.

### Read-Only

- `cloud_connectors` (Attributes List) Cloud connectors returned by the Fleet list API. Secret configuration (`vars`) is omitted from each entry. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cloud_connectors))
- `id` (String) Internal identifier for this data source read.

<a id="nestedblock--kibana_connection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `kibana_connection`

Optional:

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) API Key to use for authentication to Kibana
- `bearer_token` (String, Sensitive) Bearer Token to use for authentication to Kibana
- `ca_certs` (List of String) A list of paths to CA certificates to validate the certificate presented by the Kibana server.
- `endpoints` (List of String, Sensitive) A comma-separated list of endpoints where the terraform provider will point to, this must include the http(s) schema and port number.
- `insecure` (Boolean) Disable TLS certificate validation
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password to use for API authentication to Kibana.
- `username` (String) Username to use for API authentication to Kibana.


<a id="nestedatt--cloud_connectors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cloud_connectors`

Read-Only:

- `account_type` (String) The account type: `single-account` or `organization-account`.
- `cloud_connector_id` (String) The API-assigned cloud connector ID.
- `cloud_provider` (String) The cloud provider for this connector. One of `aws`, `azure`, or `gcp`.
- `created_at` (String) When the cloud connector was created, in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) The composite ID of the cloud connector: `<space_id>/<cloud_connector_id>`.
- `name` (String) The cloud connector name.
- `namespace` (String) The namespace assigned to this cloud connector.
- `package_policy_count` (Number) The number of package policies using this cloud connector.
- `space_id` (String) The Kibana space ID where this cloud connector is available.
- `updated_at` (String) When the cloud connector was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.
- `verification_failed_at` (String) When verification last failed, in ISO 8601 format.
- `verification_started_at` (String) When verification last started, in ISO 8601 format.
- `verification_status` (String) The verification status of the cloud connector.
